other_information.if_yes_what_sports: Varsity soccer (left wing and occasionally attacking midfield). I’ve played soccer for Navarre High School since 10th grade and have been on the varsity team since junior year. During the fall season we typically have practice Monday through Thursday from 3:00 PM to around 5:00 PM, with games usually on Tuesday and Thursday evenings. In the off-season, I also participate in optional conditioning and open-field sessions organized by the coach, focusing on sprint work, ball control, and small-sided scrimmages. Outside of school, I play in a local weekend recreational league with some of my teammates, which helps maintain fitness and chemistry. My primary role on the team is to stretch the defense on the left side, make overlapping runs, and send crosses into the box; I also track back on defense to support our left back. Last season I scored 5 goals and had 8 assists, and I’m hoping to take on more of a leadership role as a senior, especially helping younger players with positioning and game awareness.
other_information.if_yes_what_clubs:
National Honor Society (NHS): I was inducted in my junior year based on GPA and teacher recommendations. I participate in tutoring underclassmen twice a week after school, mainly in Algebra II and Chemistry, and I regularly volunteer for NHS-organized events like campus clean-up days and food drives. I’ve completed over 40 service hours through NHS, including helping at a community 5K race by setting up water stations and cleaning up afterward.

STEM Club: I attend weekly meetings where we work on small engineering and coding projects. Last semester, I was part of a group that built a simple line-following robot using Arduino and infrared sensors. We also participated in a regional STEM challenge where our team designed a bridge model out of balsa wood and tested how much weight it could hold before collapsing. I enjoy the hands-on problem-solving and collaborating with classmates who are also interested in engineering and computer science.

Spanish Club: As a heritage speaker (my dad is from Puerto Rico), I joined Spanish Club to stay connected to the language and culture in a school setting. We organize cultural events like a Hispanic Heritage Month celebration, where I helped set up a booth about Puerto Rican music and food. We also occasionally volunteer as informal translators at school events when Spanish-speaking parents attend. This has helped me feel more confident using Spanish in a more formal context and giving back to the community.
